page: Amazon Web Services a1.metal is a bare-metal server powered by the first-generation AWS Graviton processor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores using the arm64 architecture, specifically the Cortex-A72 model running at 2.3 GHz. The system contains 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and does not include any GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate multi-core capabilities alongside weaker single-core execution. The stress-ng multi-core test yields an average score of 24102.93, while the single-core score sits at 1507.99. Memory bandwidth tests show a stark contrast: cached small-block reads reach an average of 572.2 GB/sec, whereas uncached large-block reads and writes fall into the weak and poor categories at 34.01 GB/sec and 10.05 GB/sec respectively. Geekbench single-core performance is poor at 418.0, and multi-core is weak at 3520.0. However, the server performs in the average tier for software compilation with Clang and Redis SET operations, achieving 8.6 million operations per second.

The a1.metal instance offers a cost-efficient entry point into ARM-based bare-metal environments, providing dedicated physical resources without hyperthreading overhead. However, users face tradeoffs in memory-intensive workloads and single-threaded tasks, as evidenced by poor gzip compression and bzip3 single-threaded decompression speeds. It is best suited for scale-out workloads, containerized applications, web serving, and software compilation pipelines that can leverage its 16 dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services a1.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, powered by the 2.3 GHz AWS Graviton Cortex-A72 processor. Equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs, this instance is designed for ARM-native workloads. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 24102.93 and 8.6 million Redis SET operations per second, though single-core and uncached memory write speeds are relatively weak. The lack of hyperthreading provides predictable execution, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for scale-out applications, software compilation, and lightweight web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services a1.metal is a bare-metal arm64 server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps bandwidth, offering average multi-core performance for scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services a1.metal is an arm64 bare-metal server with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated arm64 bare-metal server featuring sixteen physical cores for scale-out workloads and ARM-native application development.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical arm64 cores powered by AWS Graviton Cortex-A72
32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Average multi-core performance in software compilation and Redis operations
Suited for ARM-native development, web serving, and containerized workloads
